message:
test(ftui-extras): improve metaballs.rs test coverage (bd-2t25d)

Add 23 new tests (40→63 total) covering pulse modulation, epsilon
path at ball center, multi-ball field additivity, gradient segment
boundaries, render time variation, zero-ball config, resize
grow/shrink, ball_count_for_quality edge cases, lerp_color/ping_pong
edge cases, smooth-step glow ramp, palette Hash/Eq, and quality
tier rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1oa1y","title":"Legend/Footnote Layout Primitives","description":"Background
- Link footnotes/legends need consistent, deterministic layout across sizes.
- Ad-hoc legend placement risks overlap with diagrams and breaks scaling.

Scope
- Define a generic legend layout block: placement (below/right), width allocation, and max height rules.
- Compute line wrapping and truncation for legend entries.
- Reserve and expose a legend region to the renderer without shifting diagram bounds unpredictably.
- Provide deterministic layout decisions based on size + config.
- Emit legend metrics (legend_mode, legend_height, legend_lines) for evidence logs.

Considerations
- Must be deterministic; no dynamic probing.
- Must integrate cleanly with scale tiers and diagram bounds.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for legend sizing, wrap, truncation, and placement.
- Snapshot tests for footnote legend on/off at multiple sizes.
- PTY E2E logs include legend_height and legend_mode.

Success Criteria
- Legend/footnote regions never overlap diagram content.
- Legend layout is stable across runs and sizes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueOtter","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:18.908462417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:42:54.517071134Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:42:54.517007424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oa1y","depends_on_id":"bd-25df9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:23.951514551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1oa1y","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:23.711756106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1oyeg","title":"Parser: DA/DSR device queries + character set designation (G0/G1) + single shifts","description":"## Background
The VT parser handles 56 action variants covering most core terminal sequences, but lacks several important features needed for application compatibility.

## Gaps to Fill

### Device Attribute / Status Reports (Tier 1 — critical handshake)
- DA1 (CSI c / CSI 0 c): Primary device attributes — many CLI programs send this as a startup handshake
- DA2 (CSI > c / CSI > 0 c): Secondary device attributes (terminal type/version)
- DSR (CSI 5 n): Device status report — "are you OK?" query
- CPR (CSI 6 n): Cursor position report request

### Character Set Designation (Tier 1 — affects rendering)
- G0 designate (ESC ( B / ESC ( 0): ASCII / DEC Special Graphics
- G1 designate (ESC ) B / ESC ) 0): ASCII / DEC Special Graphics
- These are used by ncurses, vim, tmux for box-drawing characters

### Single Shift (Tier 2 — completeness)
- SS2 (ESC N): Single shift to G2
- SS3 (ESC O): Single shift to G3

## Deliverables
- New Action variants for DA1, DA2, DSR, CPR, DesignateCharset, SingleShift
- Parser dispatch for all new sequences
- Unit tests for each new sequence
- Conformance fixtures for key sequences
- Wire new actions in test harnesses (no-op in harness is fine)

## Testing
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo check --all-targets
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p frankenterm-core","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:09:19.791783954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:52.264271402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:47.890125157Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1048,"issue_id":"bd-1oyeg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed: 8 new Action variants (DeviceAttributes, DeviceAttributesSecondary, DeviceStatusReport, CursorPositionReport, DesignateCharset, SingleShift2, SingleShift3), CSI dispatch for c/>c/n, ESC dispatch for (/)/*//+/N/O, EscCharset state, reply engine integration, 15 unit tests, 3 conformance fixtures. Changes landed in 500b69db.","created_at":"2026-02-09T04:17:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1oz","title":"Implement LRU width cache for text measurement","description":"# Implement LRU Width Cache for Text Measurement

## Background
Plan Section 1.4 and Appendix D.3 explicitly mention the "LRU width cache" from rich_rust as a key optimization. Text width measurement is a hot path (called for every render) and must be cached.

## Why LRU Cache?
- Unicode width calculation is expensive (grapheme segmentation + width lookup)
- Same text strings appear repeatedly (labels, status text, etc.)
- Cache hit rate expected to be high for typical UIs
- Prevents O(n) per-frame width recalculation

## Design
```rust
/// Width cache with LRU eviction
pub struct WidthCache {
    cache: LinkedHashMap<u64, usize>,  // hash -> width
    capacity: usize,
}

impl WidthCache {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LinkedHashMap::with_capacity(capacity),
            capacity,
        }
    }

    /// Get cached width or compute and cache
    pub fn get_or_compute(&mut self, text: &str, compute: impl FnOnce() -> usize) -> usize {
        let hash = hash_text(text);
        
        if let Some(&width) = self.cache.get(&hash) {
            // Move to front (LRU refresh)
            self.cache.get_refresh(&hash);
            return width;
        }
        
        let width = compute();
        
        // Evict if over capacity
        while self.cache.len() >= self.capacity {
            self.cache.pop_front();
        }
        
        self.cache.insert(hash, width);
        width
    }
    
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.cache.clear();
    }
}

// Use FNV or xxhash for fast hashing
fn hash_text(text: &str) -> u64 {
    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
    let mut hasher = FnvHasher::default();
    text.hash(&mut hasher);
    hasher.finish()
}
```

## Integration Points
- `RenderContext` holds a `RefCell<WidthCache>`
- `Text::cell_length()` uses the cache
- `Segment::cell_length()` uses the cache
- Cache is cleared on theme change (fonts can affect width)

## Cache Sizing
- Default: 1024 entries (covers typical UI with headroom)
- Configurable via `WidthCache::new(capacity)`
- Memory footprint: ~32 bytes per entry (hash + width + list pointers)

## Performance Requirements
- Cache hit: < 50ns (hash + lookup)
- Cache miss: < 5µs (grapheme segmentation + width calculation)
- Hash collision handling: rare, just recompute

## Tests Required
- [ ] Cache hits return correct cached value
- [ ] Cache misses compute and cache
- [ ] LRU eviction works correctly
- [ ] Clear() empties cache
- [ ] Thread safety if needed (RefCell for single-threaded)
- [ ] Performance benchmarks

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] LRU cache implemented with configurable capacity
- [ ] Integrated into text measurement hot path
- [ ] Performance benchmarks show significant speedup
- [ ] No correctness regressions in width measurement

## Location
`ftui-text/src/cache.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:17:08.580451576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:57:35.495094915Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:57:35.495076190Z","close_reason":"Implemented LRU width cache with FxHash, preload support, stats tracking, and optional thread-local cache. 28 new tests (117 total in crate).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oz","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ss","title":"Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility","description":"# Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility

## Background
Plan Section ADR-004 and Section 0.6.6 require a "Windows limitations" section that is honest about what works and what doesn't.

## Document Location
`docs/WINDOWS.md` or `docs/compatibility/windows.md`

## Required Content

### 1. Supported Features (v1)
Document what IS expected to work on Windows:
- [ ] Raw mode enter/exit and cleanup on panic
- [ ] Key input handling (basic keys, modifiers)
- [ ] Resize events
- [ ] Basic mouse support (where Crossterm supports it)
- [ ] Color output:
  - 16 colors (always)
  - 256 colors (Windows Terminal, modern ConHost)
  - TrueColor (Windows Terminal)

### 2. Known Limitations (v1)
Be honest about what MAY NOT work or is best-effort:
- [ ] DEC synchronized output (mode 2026): Not widely supported
- [ ] OSC 8 hyperlinks: Windows Terminal only (cmd.exe/ConHost: no)
- [ ] Bracketed paste: Varies by terminal
- [ ] Focus events: May not work in all environments
- [ ] Kitty keyboard protocol: Limited support
- [ ] Scroll-region optimization: Behavior varies
- [ ] Mouse SGR mode: May fall back to legacy encoding

### 3. Terminal Compatibility Matrix (Windows)
| Feature | Windows Terminal | cmd.exe | ConHost | PowerShell |
|---------|------------------|---------|---------|------------|
| TrueColor | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | depends |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ~ |
| Mouse | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | ~ |
| Sync Output | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |

### 4. Configuration Recommendations
- Recommend Windows Terminal for best experience
- Document any required ConPTY settings
- Explain TERM/COLORTERM environment handling on Windows

### 5. Troubleshooting Section
Common Windows issues:
- "Colors don't show": Check terminal emulator
- "Mouse not working": Enable in terminal settings
- "Cleanup not working": Known ConHost limitation
- "Unicode display broken": Font/codepage issues

### 6. Future Improvements
List what may improve in future versions:
- Better ConHost support
- WSL integration notes
- Specific terminal emulator enhancements

## Cross-References
- Link to ADR-004 for decision context
- Link to terminal compatibility matrix (bd-1un)
- Link to capability detection implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WINDOWS.md exists with all sections
- [ ] Honest about limitations (not marketing fluff)
- [ ] Tested on actual Windows systems
- [ ] Includes actionable troubleshooting
- [ ] Linked from main README.md

## Location

 {"id":"bd-2t0h","title":"Integration tests use separate lock from unit tests","description":"Integration tests (tests/*.rs) define their own local a11y_lock() at a11y_snapshots.rs:156 that doesn't coordinate with GLOBAL_A11Y_LOCK in theme.rs. This can cause race conditions when unit tests and integration tests run in parallel.

## Problem
- Unit tests use GLOBAL_A11Y_LOCK from theme.rs
- Integration tests use local a11y_lock() from a11y_snapshots.rs
- ScopedA11yLock and ScopedRenderLock are #[cfg(test)] gated, unavailable to integration tests

## Suggested Fix
1. Use #[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-support"))] instead of #[cfg(test)]
2. Add test-support feature to Cargo.toml
3. Have integration tests use the shared lock infrastructure

## Impact
Test isolation failures, potential flaky tests when running full suite","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-03T21:16:03.729677066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T21:33:38.833904243Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T21:33:38.833823901Z","close_reason":"Fixed: integration tests now use shared GLOBAL_A11Y_LOCK via ScopedA11yLock","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2tyd","title":"Implement CachedWidget<W> wrapper","description":"# Implement CachedWidget<W> wrapper

## Background
Part of Frame Caching (bd-6qxh). Cache rendered widget output to avoid re-rendering static content.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Widget wrapper that caches rendered output
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    /// Inner widget
    inner: W,
    /// Cached buffer
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    /// Last rendered area
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    /// Dirty flag (needs re-render)
    dirty: bool,
    /// Cache key (for invalidation)
    cache_key: u64,
}

/// Cached buffer with metadata
struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,
    rendered_at: Instant,
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(inner: W) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner,
            cache: None,
            last_area: None,
            dirty: true,
            cache_key: 0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Mark cache as dirty (needs re-render)
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) {
        self.dirty = true;
    }
    
    /// Invalidate if model hash changed
    pub fn invalidate_if_changed(&mut self, new_hash: u64) {
        if self.cache_key \!= new_hash {
            self.dirty = true;
            self.cache_key = new_hash;
        }
    }
    
    /// Get inner widget
    pub fn inner(&self) -> &W {
        &self.inner
    }
    
    /// Get mutable inner widget (marks dirty)
    pub fn inner_mut(&mut self) -> &mut W {
        self.dirty = true;
        &mut self.inner
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> Widget for CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget) {
        // Check if we need to re-render
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area \!= Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
        
        if needs_render {
            // Render to cache buffer
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf, budget);
            
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer {
                buffer: cache_buf,
                hash: self.cache_key,
                rendered_at: Instant::now(),
            });
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
            
            tracing::debug\!(
                widget = std::any::type_name::<W>(),
                "Cache miss - re-rendered"
            );
        } else {
            tracing::trace\!(
                widget = std::any::type_name::<W>(),
                "Cache hit"
            );
        }
        
        // Composite cache to output
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
    
    fn is_essential(&self) -> bool {
        self.inner.is_essential()
    }
}
```

## When to Use
- Static headers/footers
- Help text
- Fixed decorations
- Any widget that changes infrequently

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget wraps any Widget
- [ ] Cache used when not dirty
